title: moquinia polymorpha
intro: Moquinia polymorpha, also known as Quassia, is a tropical plant species native to the Caribbean and Central America, belonging to the Simaroubaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a natural insecticide, antimicrobial, and antifungal agent. Quassia has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and aiding in the relief of respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Quassia typically involve making tea from the leaves or bark, as well as using the plant's extracts in tinctures and capsules, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
